_comment: HUMAN-OWNED. Works that belong in the public bibliography but are not in the ORCID record — chiefly papers under review, which ORCID has no good way to represent. The sync never reads or writes this file, so entries survive every regeneration.
_howToUse: Add an entry when a paper is submitted, with `submittedTo` set to the short
When it is accepted and appears in ORCID, DELETE the entry here — the sync
will carry it from then on. Entries are matched to synced works by DOI,
arXiv id and title, so a brief overlap de-duplicates rather than double-lists.
`authors` drives the first-author/co-authored split, same as synced works.

additions.title: Confidently Wrong: Why Ignoring Binaries Biases IMF Inference at Large Sample Sizes
